title: EvaluationSession
description: Formal model of an AEP evaluation session. The session is the top-level unit of evaluation: it binds an evaluator, an agent, a test mode, injected context, one or more scenarios, and the resulting evidence into a single auditable record. Every AEP-compliant server MUST model sessions according to this schema.

Formal model of an AEP evaluation session. The session is the top-level unit of evaluation: it binds an evaluator, an agent (or a graph of agents under the CSE extension, §11.7), a test mode, injected context, one or more scenarios, and the resulting evidence into a single auditable record. Every AEP-compliant server MUST model sessions according to this schema. Exactly one of agentId or graph MUST be present (AEP-REQ-110).

sessionId.description: Server-assigned unique identifier. MUST be unpredictable (no sequential IDs).

agentId.description: The agent bound to this session for single-agent evaluation. REQUIRED unless graph is present (composite session, CSE extension).

agentVersion.description: Exact version of the agent bound to this session. REQUIRED when agentId is set. MUST be captured at session creation and MUST NOT change mid-session.

graph.description: Optional (§11.7 CSE extension). When present, this is a composite session: turns execute against nodes in the graph, not against a single agent. MUST NOT be present simultaneously with agentId. Shape defined by the CSE extension; see schemas/composite-graph.schema.json.

testMode.description: Negotiated mode for this session. Determines what visibility the evaluator has.

evaluator.description: Who is running this session. MUST be authenticated.

state.description: Current lifecycle state. Transitions MUST follow the lifecycle state machine in the normative spec.

injectedContext.description: Context supplied by the evaluator to simulate realistic conditions. Keys are the declared injection-point names from the AgentContract.

metadata.description: Session-scoped metadata. Extensions may define required keys such as redteam.

scenarios.description: Scenarios executed in this session, with status. MAY be empty for interactive sessions.

turns.description: Turns executed in this session. Agents supporting multi-turn MUST maintain ordering.

resultRef.description: Pointer to the EvaluationResult once the session is completed.

maxCostUnits.description: Abstract cost unit (tokens, currency, etc.) agreed at session creation.

InjectedContext.description: Context slots the test harness provides. Keys MUST correspond to the injection-point names declared in the AgentContract's contextModel. The payload shape for each key is validated against that injection point's schema.

transitions.description: Ordered record of state transitions. MUST be immutable once written.
